title: Ph sensing using pseudo-graphite
abstract: Methods, electrodes, and sensors for pH sensing using pseudo-graphite are disclosed. In one illustrative embodiment, a method may include coating a pseudo-graphite material onto a surface of an electrode substrate to produce a pseudo-graphite surface. The method may also include exposing the pseudo-graphite surface to a sample to measure a pH of the sample.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method comprising: coating a pseudo-graphite material onto a surface of an electrode substrate to produce a pseudo-graphite surface, and exposing the pseudo-graphite surface to a sample to measure a pH of the sample. 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising modifying the pseudo-graphite surface with an electrochemically sensitive chemistry to alter a sensing property of the electrode to enhance the electrode for pH measurement. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein modifying the pseudo-graphite surface comprises attaching oxygen-based chemistries to the pseudo-graphite surface to enhance the electrode for pH measurement. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein attaching oxygen-based chemistries to the pseudo-graphite surface comprises attaching the oxygen-based chemicals through in-situ oxidation. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein attaching oxygen-based chemistries to the pseudo-graphite surface comprises applying an oxidizing electrical potential to the pseudo graphite surface. 6. The method of claim 3, wherein attaching oxygen-based chemistries to the pseudo-graphite surface comprises applying an oxidizing electrical potential to the pseudo graphite surface. 7. The method of claim 1, further comprising measuring pH with the electrode through at least one of square wave voltammetry or sweep voltammetry. 8. The method of claim 1, further comprising measuring pH with the electrode through open circuit potential. 9. The method of claim 1, further comprising measuring pH with the electrode in conjunction with localized pH modulation. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the pseudo-graphite has fast heterogeneous electron transfer at a basal plane. 11. The method of claim 1, wherein the pseudo-graphite has a corrosion resistance greater than graphitic materials. 12. The method of any preceding claim, wherein a carbon content of the pseudo graphite includes 80-90% sp2 carbon and 10-20% sp3 carbon. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein the pseudo-graphite has a corrosion resistance greater than graphitic materials, and wherein the pseudo-graphite has fast heterogeneous electron transfer at a basal plane. 14. The method of any one of claims 1-9, wherein the pseudo-graphite has a corrosion resistance greater than graphitic materials, and wherein the pseudo-graphite has fast heterogeneous electron transfer at a basal plane. 15. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising measuring pH with the electrode through at least one of square wave voltammetry or sweep voltammetry. 16. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising measuring pH with the electrode through open circuit potential. 17. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising measuring pH with the electrode in conjunction with localized pH modulation. 18. An electrode comprising: an electrode substrate with a surface, and a pseudo-graphite material coated onto the surface of the electrode substrate to produce a pseudo-graphite surface for pH measurement. 19. The electrode of claim 18, wherein the pseudo-graphite has been modified with an electrochemically sensitive chemistry to alter a sensing property of the electrode to enhance the electrode for pH measurement. 20. The electrode of claim 18, wherein oxygen-based chemistries are attached to the pseudo-graphite surface to enhance the electrode for pH measurement. 21. The electrode of claim 20, wherein the oxygen-based chemistries are attached through in-situ oxidation. 22. The electrode of claim 21, wherein the oxidizing electrical potential is applied to the pseudo-graphite surface to attach the oxygen-based chemistries. 23. The electrode of claim 20, wherein the oxidizing electrical potential is applied to the pseudo-graphite surface to attach the oxygen-based chemistries. 24. The electrode of claim 18, wherein the pseudo-graphite has a fast heterogeneous electron transfer at a basal plane. 25. The electrode of claim 18, wherein the pseudo-graphite has a corrosion resistance greater than graphitic materials. 26. The electrode of any one of claims 18-25, wherein a carbon content of the pseudo graphite includes 80-90% sp2 carbon and 10-20% sp3 carbon. 27. The electrode of claim 26, wherein the pseudo-graphite has a corrosion resistance greater than graphitic materials, and wherein the pseudo-graphite has fast heterogeneous electron transfer at a basal plane. 28. The electrode of any one of claims 18-23, wherein the pseudo-graphite has a corrosion resistance greater than graphitic materials, and wherein the pseudo-graphite has fast heterogeneous electron transfer at a basal plane. 29. A sensor comprising: an electrode, an electrical source to supply at least one of a current or voltage to the electrode, and a measurement circuit to measure a resultant signal from the electrode, wherein the electrode includes: an electrode substrate with a surface, and a pseudo-graphite material coated onto the surface of the electrode substrate to produce a pseudo-graphite surface, wherein the pseudo-graphite surface is modified with an electrochemically sensitive chemistry to alter a sensing property of the electrode to enhance the electrode for pH measurement. 30. The sensor of claim 29, wherein oxygen-based chemistries are attached to the pseudo-graphite surface to enhance the electrode for pH measurement. 31. The sensor of claim 30, wherein the oxygen-based chemistries are attached through in-situ oxidation. 32. The sensor of claim 31, wherein the oxidizing electrical potential is applied to the pseudo-graphite surface to attach the oxygen-based chemistries. 33.
